cifrado-homomorfico.module_doc:
Homomorphic encryption: minimal example with TenSEAL.

Encrypts a list of salaries, computes the mean over the encrypted data,
and decrypts the result. At no point does the computation access the
plaintext data. Compares performance between FHE and plaintext computation.

Requirements: pip install tenseal
cifrado-homomorfico.crear_contexto_doc: Creates an FHE context with CKKS scheme parameters.
cifrado-homomorfico.media_fhe_doc: Computes the mean over encrypted data. Returns (result, timings).
cifrado-homomorfico.varianza_fhe_doc:
Computes the population variance over encrypted data.

Uses Var(X) = E[X²] - (E[X])². For the subtraction to work in CKKS,
both terms must follow the same operation path
(one ciphertext×ciphertext multiplication, zero scalar multiplications).
The trick: encrypt a pre-scaled copy by 1/n.
cifrado-homomorfico.regresion_fhe_doc:
Linear regression prediction over encrypted features.

The model weights (in plaintext) are multiplied by the features
(encrypted) and summed — a homomorphic dot product.
The server never sees the patient's data.
cifrado-homomorfico.busqueda_fhe_doc:
Retrieves a record by index without revealing which one is queried (PIR).

The client encrypts a one-hot vector with a 1 at the desired position.
The server multiplies that vector by the database (in plaintext)
and sums — obtaining the selected value, encrypted.
The server never knows which position was queried.
cifrado-homomorfico.muro_del_ruido_doc:
Demonstrates what happens when the level budget is exceeded.

Creates a context with a single multiplicative level and attempts
two chained multiplications. The second one fails.
cifrado-homomorfico.error_segunda_mult: ERROR: the second multiplication should not work
cifrado-homomorfico.servidor_msg: The server processed ALL records without knowing which one was requested.
cifrado-homomorfico.bootstrap_module_doc:
CKKS Bootstrapping with OpenFHE.

Demonstrates how bootstrapping restores the multiplicative levels
of an exhausted ciphertext, allowing continued operations.
It is the solution to the 'noise wall' we saw with TenSEAL in quick_start.py.

Requirements: pip install openfhe
cifrado-homomorfico.crear_contexto_bootstrap_doc:
Creates a CKKS context with bootstrapping enabled.

Args:
    niveles_utiles: multiplications available between bootstraps.
    num_slots: number of values per ciphertext.

Returns:
    (cc, keys, profundidad): cryptographic context, keys, and
    total configured depth.
cifrado-homomorfico.bootstrapping_demo_doc: Replicates Recipe 4 (noise wall) and overcomes it with bootstrapping.
